purpose: Visualize hash-chained run ledgers, traces, and agent trajectories; back deterministic eval cases with a queryable store.

phoenix.strength: Deep agent trajectory evaluation; pytest/vitest integration; span-based tracing.
phoenix.notes: Recommended when deep agent behavior evaluation is the priority.

langfuse.strength: Full LLM observability stack: trace, metrics, evals, prompt management, datasets, playground.
langfuse.notes: Recommended when prompt management and self-hosted data sovereignty are priorities.

helicone.strength: Lightweight proxy-mode observability; minimal code changes; cost and latency monitoring.
helicone.notes: Minimum-intrusion option when Phoenix/Langfuse are too heavy.

integration_points.run_ledger: Every hash-chained run ledger entry emits a trace span with run_id, cycle, stage, and controller_decision.
integration_points.model_calls: litellm_gateway emits OpenTelemetry spans for each model call; consumed by configured backend.
integration_points.handoffs: Typed handoff envelopes emit spans with from_agent, to_agent, and contract_hash.
integration_points.eval_cases: Deterministic eval cases are stored as datasets in the configured backend for regression runs.

selection_guidance: Configure one primary backend. Phoenix for agent-eval depth; Langfuse for full-stack + prompt management; Helicone for lightweight proxy mode. All three are self-hostable.
